title: Campaign: production restore + live prod recall proof
description:
Operator directive (QUEUE.md 2026-07-02T20:07). After recall-v2 reaches terminal state, restore the production Sinex runtime. The TimescaleDB compression-lock fix is on master but the restore has never been executed.

Sequence: (1) read-only verification plan first — data integrity of the 74M events, then service bring-up order; (2) execute as explicit operator-visible control-plane operation; (3) prove: production sinexd healthy, event counts match pre-incident expectations, one recall query answered from prod data.

Every external "live substrate" claim is gated on this. Note prod boot robustness work in gh#2182-remaining (schema-apply idempotent trigger guards) — check it before bring-up; the boot-race can recur via CAGG refresh policies racing DROP/CREATE TRIGGER.

Refs: QUEUE.md, gh#2182 comment trail, prod snapshot /var/lib/sinex/.prewipe-snapshot-20260627 (kept-data recovery, no wipe).
design: Read-only restore readiness packet written: .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-sinex-mhk-prod-restore-readiness.md. Current evidence: prod postgresql/nats/sinexd inactive; PGDATA /var/lib/sinex/postgresql/18 exists, PG_VERSION=18, postmaster.pid absent, pg_controldata reports cluster state 'shut down' with latest checkpoint 2026-06-30T02:57:35, data checksums enabled; journal shows PostgreSQL clean shutdown and NATS clean shutdown, while sinexd timed out and was SIGKILLed after module drain. Schema boot-race prerequisite is handled by 8f25c3eb0 / sinex-yss. Pending acceptance: exact ~74M row-count proof, prod sinexd health, and one recall query from prod require an explicit operator-visible start.
acceptance_criteria: prod sinexd healthy; event counts match pre-incident expectations (~74M); one recall query answered from prod data; restore executed as an explicit operator-visible operation.
notes:
2026-07-03 runtime restore found a second production blocker after disk-backpressure fix: sinexd skipped stream bootstrap because SINEX_NATS_STREAMS_MANAGED_EXTERNALLY=true, but Nix-managed NATS topology omitted PROD_SINEX_RAW_EVENTS_CONFIRMED / prod.events.confirmed.>. Event engine persisted rows then failed fatal confirmation publish with 'no stream found for given subject'. Fix in progress: add confirmed-events stream and ACL subject to nixos/modules/nats.nix, switch, then restart and verify no confirmation_durability_gap.
2026-07-03 continuation: production restore reached healthy serving state after fixing and deploying multiple blockers. Sinex commits involved: e4faedebe schema apply hardening, e729745fa disk-backpressure requires low free space, 77c129ff9 confirmed-events stream provisioning, f3c3b69ac static git safe.directory env. Sinnix commits involved: eed0c1a confirmed stream provisioning and fb93822 persistent source-access repair + service-level Git safe.directory env. Deployed with SINNIX_SINEX_OVERRIDE=/realm/project/sinex nix develop --command switch, then restarted sinexd. Post-restart proof: systemctl shows active/running main PID 745417; service unit has GIT_CONFIG safe.directory=/realm/project/sinex; sudo -u sinex can read Atuin, Chrome, qutebrowser history DBs; journal after restart has no new dubious ownership / unable to open database file / git log exited errors. Recovery action: raw consumer was pinned at ack_floor 525 behind stale sensing/missing materials and DLQ pressure; sampled raw sequences showed mixed stale half-material shell/browser, orphaned ActivityWatch, and self-telemetry. Purged already-poisoned transport backlog intentionally to establish fresh realtime boundary: nats stream purge PROD_SINEX_RAW_EVENTS removed 11631 messages; nats stream purge PROD_SINEX_RAW_EVENTS_DLQ removed 836 messages. This drops recovery-prefix raw messages, including some recoverable half-built/fresh telemetry, and is recorded as an intentional restore tradeoff. Post-purge proof: sinexctl runtime health returned healthy=true, raw_ingest_dlq empty; PROD_SINEX_RAW_EVENTS pending=0 ack_pending=3 redelivered=0 shortly after purge; PROD_SOURCE_MATERIAL pending=0 ack_pending=4; DB/NATS/confirmation/replay/SSE components healthy. Remaining follow-up: source-material assembler still reports active sensing rotating materials and occasional long lock-hold warnings; old failed/recovered_partial materials remain historical residue; implement/prioritize paced drain, source-material defrag/cleanup, storage-growth, and DLQ/root-cause analytics Beads rather than relying on manual purge next time.
close_reason: Completed by production restore and proof packet .agent/demos/sinex/prod-restore-recall-proof-20260703T095311Z. Evidence: deployed local Sinex/Sinnix fixes, restarted sinexd.service, runtime health healthy=true/serving=true with DB/NATS/raw-ingest DLQ/confirmation/replay/SSE healthy; raw consumer caught up pending=0 ack_pending=0 redelivered=0; core.events Timescale chunk estimate 80,872,976 rows, matching/exceeding the pre-incident ~74M expectation and consistent with the earlier exact restore count around 80.8M; source_material_registry exact 34,782; persisted DLQ exact 0. Live prod recall proof: sinexctl recall --rpc-url https://127.0.0.1:9999 --window 5m --limit 10 --include-self-observation --format json returned in ~140ms with 12 events across 5 sources, including activitywatch and git material-provenance rows. Residual broad recall latency is tracked separately as sinex-0tj; source-material debt/defrag remains under existing robustness work.

title: Operator & agent UX: make Sinex tangible — the daily surface, the judgment seat, the agent hookup
description: Operator directive (2026-07-03): UX revamp making Sinex tangible for the operator AND for agents. This epic also owns, by operator ruling, the two strategic risks previously unowned: the judgment bottleneck (adjudication is the only path to truth and has no workflow) and the consumption-habit loop (tower complete but unused is the failure mode). Diagnosis: every capability currently terminates in a JSON-emitting CLI invocation the operator must remember to type and an agent must be separately taught. The system has no place you LOOK, no place you JUDGE, and no default way agents arrive already-connected. Three children: (1) the operator daily surface, (2) the adjudication seat, (3) agent arrival. Latency is a prerequisite handled elsewhere (sinex-0tj/g3k); demo polish is cem's; this epic is the standing product surfaces.
design: PRINCIPLES (settled): tangible = zero-recall-cost entry points (a keybind, a pane, a morning artifact, an ambient hook) — not more commands; every surface renders through the shared card/ref substrate (no UI-only queries — algebra rule); every number shown carries provenance or is absent; gaps render as gaps. Agents are first-class users: the same surfaces must be MCP/JSON-addressable, and agent-facing descriptions teach the data model (materials/derived/refs) so a cold agent uses refs correctly without repo docs.
acceptance_criteria: Operator reaches for a Sinex surface unprompted on an ordinary day (the habit test — measurable via consumption telemetry, cf sinex-nbi.2); proposals get judged within days not never (queue age visible); new agent sessions arrive with Sinex context without per-session setup.

title: Recover zero-event orphaned source materials without raw DLQ
description: After deploying the zero-event slice_arrival_timeout remediation, production health still degraded from PROD_SINEX_RAW_EVENTS_DLQ because reconcile_orphaned_sensing_materials routed an ordinary zero-event desktop.window-manager orphan as orphaned_sensing_material. That is the same empty source-material residue class under the restart/orphan path: status failed, parsed_event_count=0, total_bytes NULL, no useful raw event to retry. Make zero-event ordinary orphaned sensing materials recovered_partial with explicit metadata/remediation instead of raw DLQ pressure.
acceptance_criteria: Orphaned ordinary source material with parsed_event_count=0 is marked recovered_partial and does not publish events.dlq.event_engine; remediation_plan shows a distinct inspect_empty_orphaned_source_material decision for the recovery reason; eventful ordinary orphans still route as explicit failures; targeted sinexd tests and check pass.
close_reason: Implemented in working tree and verified before commit: zero-event ordinary orphaned sensing materials now mark recovered_partial with recovery_reason orphaned_zero_event_source_material_recovered_partial and dlq_policy suppressed_zero_event_source_material_restart_orphan, while eventful ordinary orphans keep explicit failure routing. API remediation_plan maps that recovery reason to inspect_empty_orphaned_source_material. Verification: targeted sinexd test job 2001365 passed, including orphan_reconcile_recovers_globally_stale_zero_event_source_material_without_dlq and remediation_decision_inspects_empty_orphaned_source_material; xtask check -p sinexd passed as job 2001366.

title: Fix sources archive to scope lifecycle archival to the requested material
description: While implementing sinex-17w, source-material remediation needed a safe operator clear path. Existing sinexctl sources archive <material_id> is not safe to advertise for that purpose: handle_sources_archive verifies the requested source material exists and counts events for it, but for non-dry-run it builds LifecycleArchiveRequest with before/source/event_ids all None and limit=10000, so the underlying lifecycle archive is not scoped to the requested material. Fix the RPC to archive exactly the requested material cascade or remove/guard the non-dry-run path until it is precise.
acceptance_criteria: sources.archive non-dry-run archives only the requested material and its derived cascade; dry-run preview and execution use the same scoped cascade; regression proves another material's events are not archived; remediation guidance may safely point at sources archive only after this is true.
close_reason: Fixed sources.archive to collect root event ids for the requested source material and feed those ids into lifecycle archive for both dry-run and execution. Regression proves the requested material root plus derived cascade archive while another material remains live. Verified with xtask test -p sinexd -E 'test(sources_archive_scopes_to_requested_material_and_derived_cascade)' (job 2001367) and xtask check -p sinexd (job 2001368).

title: Make material-assembler slice timeouts health-actionable instead of DLQ noise
description: During production restore on 2026-07-03, nine zero-event self-observation materials created around restart/purge cleanup timed out with slice_arrival_timeout and were published to PROD_SINEX_RAW_EVENTS_DLQ. Raw ingestion was caught up, but health degraded solely because these assembly-failure records sat in the raw-ingest DLQ until manually purged. The system needs a clearer distinction between event-ingest DLQ pressure and material-assembly cleanup residue, plus an operator action/remediation path that does not require ad hoc NATS stream purges.
acceptance_criteria: A slice_arrival_timeout for a stale zero-event material is surfaced through a material-assembly health/debt/remediation channel with material_id/source/status/count context; raw-ingest DLQ health no longer conflates these records with event ingestion failures; operator can clear or acknowledge resolved assembly residues through sinexctl/xtask without direct nats stream purge; tests cover stale zero-event self-observation material timeout classification.
notes: Evidence: after sinex-mhk restore proof, health degraded with raw-ingest DLQ pressure: 9 pending. PROD_SINEX_RAW_EVENTS_DLQ seq 837..845 decoded to {material_id, error:slice_arrival_timeout, elapsed_seconds:359, timeout_seconds:3600}; corresponding raw.source_material_registry rows were failed, parsed_event_count=0, total_bytes NULL, sources were sinex.self-observation.* materials started around 2026-07-03T09:38 and failed at 09:50. Raw event consumer remained caught up. Manual purge restored healthy runtime.
close_reason: Completed by c37b533b9. Zero-event slice_arrival_timeout materials now bypass raw-ingest DLQ and land as recovered_partial source-material remediation with material/source/count metadata; self-observation residue is low-severity acknowledgement, ordinary source-material residue is medium-severity inspection. Operator acknowledgement is through the existing safe per-material command sinexctl sources annotate <material_id> --tag acknowledged-empty-timeout. Verification: material-assembler focused job 2001359 passed 2 tests; remediation handler job 2001361 passed 4 tests; xtask check -p sinexd passed job 2001362 with no current sinexd diagnostics.

comments.author: Sinity
comments.text:
Research packet written: `.agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-sinex-17w-assembler-dlq-health.md`.

Exact current DLQ path:
- `crate/sinexd/src/event_engine/material_assembler/maintenance.rs:250-288` — `MaterialAssembler::find_stale_materials` identifies stale in-memory assemblies by `slice_arrival_timeout`.
- `crate/sinexd/src/event_engine/material_assembler/maintenance.rs:332-375` — `MaterialAssembler::process_stale_material` calls `mark_timeout_material_recovered_partial_if_eventful(..., "slice_arrival_timeout")`; if that returns false/error it calls `route_material_error(material_id, "slice_arrival_timeout", {timeout_seconds, elapsed_seconds})` and then `finalize_failed_material(material_id, "slice_arrival_timeout")`.
- `crate/sinexd/src/event_engine/material_assembler/finalize.rs:132-196` — `MaterialAssembler::route_material_error` builds `MaterialDlqPayload { material_id, error, context, failed_at }`, tags it `NatsTrafficClass::RawIngestDlq` plus semantic `transport::Class::SourceMaterial`, and publishes to `self.dlq_subject`.
- `crate/sinexd/src/event_engine/material_assembler/finalize.rs:220-271` — `mark_timeout_material_recovered_partial_if_eventful` only suppresses the raw-DLQ path for `slice_arrival_timeout` when `parsed_event_count > 0`; zero-event materials return `Ok(false)` and fall through to DLQ.
- `crate/sinexd/src/event_engine/material_assembler/mod.rs:337-339` — `self.dlq_subject` is `events.dlq.event_engine` under the runtime namespace, covered by the raw-ingest DLQ stream.

Exact health classification path:
- `crate/sinexd/src/api/service_container.rs:53-59` — `RawIngestDlqHealth` is the gateway component.
- `crate/sinexd/src/api/service_container.rs:405-510` — `ServiceContainer::probe_raw_ingest_dlq_pressure` inspects `SINEX_RAW_EVENTS_DLQ`; `state.messages == 0` is Healthy, any non-zero message count is Degraded with detail `raw-ingest DLQ pressure: ...`.
- `crate/sinexd/src/api/service_container.rs:656-678` — `health_report` pushes that detail into `degradation_reasons` when raw-ingest DLQ is Degraded.
- `crate/sinexd/src/api/service_container.rs:688-693` — top-level `healthy` is false when raw-ingest DLQ is Degraded.
- `crate/sinexd/src/api/handlers/system.rs:33-77` — `system_health_response` exposes the component status/detail as `components.raw_ingest_dlq`.

Existing adjacent surfaces:
- `crate/sinexd/src/event_engine/material_assembler/maintenance.rs:377-467` already suppresses DLQ for globally stale orphaned self-observation registry rows by marking them `recovered_partial` with `dlq_policy = "suppressed_self_observation_restart_orphan"`.
- `crate/sinexd/src/api/handlers/sources.rs:371-448` implements `sources.remediation_plan`; `sources.rs:399-400` excludes empty failed materials unless `include_empty` is true.
- `crate/sinexctl/src/commands/sources.rs:413-484` exposes `sinexctl sources remediation-plan --include-empty`.
- `crate/sinexctl/src/commands/ops/debt.rs:171-184` and `crate/sinexctl/src/commands/ops/catchup.rs:90-99` call `sources.remediation_plan` with `include_empty: false`, so zero-event residue is not shown in those dashboards today.

Small first implementation slice:
1. In `MaterialAssembler::process_stale_material`, before `route_material_error`, classify zero-event self-observation timeouts as material remediation debt: read `source_identifier`/`parsed_event_count`; if source is `sinex.self-observation.*` (including `#material=` suffix) and count is 0, mark `recovered_partial` instead of publishing to raw DLQ.
2. Use a recovery reason like `slice_arrival_timeout_zero_event_self_observation_recovered_partial` and metadata with `material_id`, `source_identifier`, `elapsed_seconds`, `timeout_seconds`, `parsed_event_count: 0`, `dlq_policy: "suppressed_zero_event_self_observation_timeout"`.
3. Reuse source-material remediation surfaces: update `remediation_decision` for that recovery reason to low severity review/acknowledge, and optionally include empty recovered-partial material residue in `ops debt` / `ops catchup` or expose a separate count.
4. Defer raw-DLQ health filtering by semantic transport class for a later slice. `probe_raw_ingest_dlq_pressure` currently does only cheap stream-state inspection; peeking/classifying messages in the health path is a larger latency-sensitive change.

Suggested tests:
- `crate/sinexd/src/event_engine/material_assembler/maintenance_test.rs`: add `stale_zero_event_self_observation_timeout_recovers_partial_without_dlq`, mirroring existing `stale_eventful_timeout_recovers_partial_without_dlq` but with `parsed_event_count = 0` and a `sinex.self-observation.*#material=<id>` source; assert `RecoveredPartial`, metadata, and no `events.dlq.event_engine` message.
- Same file: optional counter-test that zero-event non-self-observation timeout still routes to DLQ and marks failed.
- `crate/sinexd/src/api/handlers/sources_test.rs`: add remediation-decision test for the new recovery reason mapping to low severity review/acknowledge.
- `crate/sinexctl/src/commands/ops_test.rs` or `crate/sinexctl/src/commands/sources_test.rs`: if debt/catchup visibility changes, assert the row appears with the new reason.

Verification commands for implementation:
```bash
xtask test -p sinexd -E 'test(stale_zero_event_self_observation_timeout_recovers_partial_without_dlq)'
xtask test -p sinexd -E 'test(remediation_decision)'
xtask test -p sinexctl -E 'test(source_remediation_plan)'
xtask check -p sinexd
xtask check -p sinexctl
```
Do not use bare cargo. Reserve `xtask check --full` for final implementation confidence, not research.
